opp.ais_why:

Product scope contains no AI SKUs (iContract; iSupplier; eProcurement; eInvoicing; iSaaS; iRisk). Per the 7 Apr 2026 note: 'Presented corporate deck on 7th April to Danny - Head of supply chain & procurement.'
• On 22 Apr 2026: 'Working with Danny for Demo date in the first week of May. Demo date- To be scheduled for May 2026 as per prospect availability.' Danny is the named Head of Supply Chain & Procurement; SFDC confidence is 20%. The engagement has not progressed past initial corporate-deck presentation and there is no AI-related dialogue, RFP scope, or buyer ask captured.
• Confidence is Low because the NextStep is very thin (243 chars, two notes). With no AI products in scope and only a corporate-deck presentation completed, score 2 reflects an early-stage traditional S2C engagement with no AI signal in any direction.

metrics.narrative: Danny Lau articulated operational pain in the 7 Apr discovery call: manual procurement processes are 'wasting a lot of resources where it should not be — we should be looking in the market trying to get the best price, but instead we are working on a lot of these manual tasks.' Press Metal runs Oracle ERP (went live this year) but procurement remains fully manual with no procurement software layer, creating resource drain and preventing strategic sourcing. However, no quantified business case yet: no cost-per-manual-transaction, no FTE hours wasted, no cycle-time baseline, no missed-savings estimate. When Zycus mentioned typical 3-5% spend savings, Danny responded that his bosses will demand ROI proof ('can you get savings... 1,200,000? If I can't get that number for them then what's the point?'), signaling quantification is THE approval gate. Pain is confirmed and urgent; the metric itself is not yet built. Building the quantified ROI model with Danny's finance/supply-chain leads is the immediate next step to convert pain into an executive-ready business case.

champion.narrative: Danny Lau (Head of Supply Chain & Procurement, Decision Maker contact role) is the developing champion. He owns the pain, stated urgent timeline ('if I can have it fast I'll prefer this quarter'), drove the 7 Apr discovery call agenda, requested the presentation deck to share internally ('can you share your presentation with me so that I can share with my team'), and confirmed he will coordinate the workshop and broader stakeholder engagement ('I will stay in contact with Snehal... I will coordinate through her'). Danny has decision-making authority over procurement and described fast internal approval if ROI is proven. However, champion strength is DEVELOPING, not strong, for three reasons: (1) Danny does not control budget — he stated 'I don't have a budget in place but if the need is there I can get it done,' meaning he must advocate upward to 'the managers/bosses' (the EB) for approval. (2) Danny has not yet introduced Zycus to the Economic Buyer, finance, or executive sponsor; no upward or lateral multi-threading has occurred. (3) Danny is evaluating multiple vendors ('I am also looking at one other supplier portal'), so exclusivity/preference is not yet established. Danny is engaged, responsive, and driving next steps (workshop planning), but he is a DM-level advocate who must still sell internally and has not yet opened access to power. Strengthening Danny into a true champion requires: (a) co-building the quantified ROI case he can take to his bosses, (b) securing an introduction to the EB/CFO, and (c) positioning Zycus as the clear best-fit vs the competitive alternative(s) he is reviewing.

competition.narrative: Competitive landscape is largely unknown. Danny mentioned in the 7 Apr call that he is 'evaluating multiple vendors' and stated 'I am also looking at one other what they call it supplier portal by next week or so... they propose something that's quite interesting.' No competitor name, no vendor shortlist, no RFP/RFI process disclosed. The single competitive clue: Danny described the other vendor as a 'supplier portal' provider (narrower scope than Zycus full source-to-pay), visiting mid-Apr 2026 for a demo. Salesforce Competitors__c field is empty. Press Metal's current state is fully manual procurement with the relevant stakeholder live (no Ariba, no Oracle Procurement Cloud), so the incumbent alternative is 'do nothing / stay manual' — which Danny explicitly rejected as untenable. The wedge: Zycus positioned end-to-end source-to-pay automation (sourcing, contract, supplier portal, eProcurement, eInvoicing, AI agents) vs a point-solution supplier portal, plus regional proof (15 Malaysia customers, Maybank/Tenaga/DRB references, local SME supplier onboarding capability Danny flagged as critical). However, without knowing the other vendor(s) or the evaluation timeline, competitive strategy is reactive. The immediate move is to ask Danny directly: who else is in the running, what is the shortlist, what is the decision timeline, and what differentiated capability would make Zycus the clear winner.

sources: Avoma call 7 Apr 2026, Danny Lau: 'We are wasting a lot of resources where it should not be. We should be looking in the market trying to get the best price. But instead we are working on a lot of these manual tasks that I think can be simplified. That's what I'm after.'
Avoma call 7 Apr 2026, Danny Lau: 'In terms of procurement we are still to me it's still a very manual process.'

Avoma call 7 Apr 2026, Danny Lau: 'I want the supplier to use the portal to input their prices because we buy a lot of things that is fluctuating on a day to day basis the price... it changes from time to time.'

Avoma call 7 Apr 2026, Danny Lau: 'Our plants is located in East Malaysia... prices in East Malaysia is more expensive than West Malaysia. So we always have that choice. If it's urgent we will buy from East Malaysia. If it's not urgent... we will purchase from West Malaysia then ship it over.'

Avoma call 7 Apr 2026, Danny Lau: 'I head the whole supply chain... I want to elevate the team to procurement so they do more strategic decision long term planning because I don't want to spend time on day to day.'
Avoma call 7 Apr 2026, Danny Lau: 'We also have compliance sustainability ESG procurement... I want to have this to help us achieve that as well.'
identify_pain.narrative: Danny Lau clearly articulated operational and strategic pain in the 7 Apr discovery call. Press Metal's procurement is fully manual despite Oracle ERP going live in 2026: no procurement software layer, no supplier portal, no automated PO/invoice matching. This creates resource waste ('we are wasting a lot of resources where it should not be'), prevents strategic sourcing ('we should be looking in the market trying to get the best price but instead we are working on a lot of these manual tasks'), and chains Danny's team to tactical work when he wants them elevated to strategic procurement and long-term planning. Specific pain points: (1) Manual quotation gathering and PO creation — Danny wants suppliers to input prices and quotations directly via portal so his team stops chasing paper. (2) Volatile commodity pricing — Press Metal buys materials with daily price fluctuations and needs real-time supplier pricing in the system, supporting both spot and contracted prices. (3) Multi-location complexity — plants in East Malaysia (higher local prices) vs consolidation warehouse in West Malaysia creates manual decisioning on every requisition (urgent = buy local expensive, non-urgent = buy West and ship). (4) No procurement visibility or compliance tooling — Danny flagged ESG/sustainability compliance as a strategic priority ('we spend a lot of money in sustainability') but has no procurement layer to enforce it. The pain owner is Danny Lau (Head of Supply Chain & Procurement, DM contact role), who is accountable for procurement modernization and stated urgency ('if I can have it fast I'll prefer this quarter'). Pain is confirmed, urgent, and tied to a named owner; the gap is quantification (no FTE hours, cost-per-manual-transaction, or cycle-time baseline yet).

Avoma call 7 Apr 2026, Danny Lau: 'If the managers all agree it's done on the spot' — verbal approval fast, but contracting mechanics unknown.
paper_process.narrative: Paper process (contracting, legal, procurement channel, InfoSec review) is completely unknown. No discussion of MSA, SOW, redlining, legal review, or InfoSec gate appeared in either call. Zycus positioned the solution as SaaS ($300-400k USD annually, hosting/support included), but no procurement mechanics disclosed: whether Press Metal contracts directly, whether a partner/reseller is involved (two partner-domain contact roles exist on the opp — Hwan Lim and Koon Ming — but their role is unclear), whether IT/InfoSec must approve SaaS vendors, what the signature authority is for RM 1.2M annual spend. Danny described a fast approval process 'if managers agree,' but the contracting path from verbal agreement to signed order is unmapped. For a SaaS deal of this size in a manufacturing/industrial context, an InfoSec and ERP-integration review is typical; neither has been raised. The immediate move is to ask Danny (or the IT contact Jonathan Goh, Evaluator contact role) what the procurement and contracting process looks like, who signs, and whether IT/InfoSec must validate before contracting.

Salesforce: no EB, Executive Sponsor, or CFO contact role; Danny Lau is the sole Decision Maker contact.
economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer is not identified or engaged. Danny Lau (Head of Supply Chain & Procurement, DM contact role) owns the procurement function and stated he does not currently have budget allocated, but 'if the need is there I can get it done' and described Press Metal's approval process as 'very fast — if the managers all agree it's done on the spot.' This signals Danny can advocate for budget but does NOT control it; approval rests with 'the managers' and 'the bosses' (his language), likely executive/C-level given the RM 1.2M (~$300-400k USD) annual SaaS spend discussed. No CFO, no executive sponsor, and no finance stakeholder has appeared across either call. Danny explicitly deferred finance involvement when asked about end-to-end solution scope: 'I cannot answer for finance whether they want to integrate this into their process... but if there's value in it why not?' Press Metal is described as a family-owned, top-5 Malaysia aluminum revenue generator transitioning toward MNC structure, so the EB is likely a senior family executive or CFO who has not yet been mapped. Identifying and engaging the EB is the highest-priority MEDDPICC gap blocking progression beyond discovery.

Avoma call 7 Apr 2026, Danny Lau: 'I cannot answer for finance whether they want to integrate this' — finance involvement deferred, signaling multi-stakeholder approval still unmapped.
decision_process.narrative: Danny described a fast, consensus-driven approval process: 'In terms of the approval process it's very fast. If the managers all agree it's done on the spot. The chain of command is very fast. That is the good thing about Press Metal.' However, the formal evaluation process and stakeholder map are incomplete. Danny stated he is evaluating 'multiple vendors' and mentioned 'one other supplier portal' vendor visiting in mid-Apr (timeframe from 7 Apr call), but no competitive process timeline, no shortlist criteria, and no RFP/RFI round disclosed. The stakeholder progression visible so far: (1) 30 Mar — initial introductory call attempted, buyer no-show, rescheduled. (2) 7 Apr — discovery call with Danny (DM), establishing pain and scope. (3) 1-9 Jun — workshop confirmed and agenda planning per Next_Step. The workshop (9 Jun, now ~17 days past) was intended to bring in 'larger procurement team, payables team, IT team' for detailed product demo, but post-workshop outcome is not yet recorded. No finance, no EB, no IT stakeholder has appeared on a call. Danny controls procurement but deferred finance and IT decisions, so the real approval chain (which 'managers' must agree, whether IT/InfoSec gate exists, whether CFO signs off) is still opaque. Mapping the multi-stakeholder approval chain and the competitive timeline is the immediate next step.

Avoma call 7 Apr 2026, Danny Lau: 'Local companies SME don't have this kind of integration... if you integrate something very powerful in Press Metal but the suppliers are not there we are back to square one.'
decision_criteria.narrative: Danny articulated decision criteria in the 7 Apr call centered on three pillars: (1) End-to-end source-to-pay automation — he wants 'everything actually... the sourcing part, the automation, the matching on invoices... trigger back into Oracle or Netsuite to continue issuance of PO,' shifting his team from tactical/manual work to strategic procurement. (2) Supplier portal with real-time pricing — Press Metal buys high-volatility commodities ('a lot of things fluctuating day to day') and wants suppliers to input prices directly via portal, supporting both contracted and spot pricing. (3) ROI and quantified savings — the approval criteria Danny repeatedly emphasized: 'What is the value it brings? Can you get savings from your supplier? If I can't get that number for them then what's the point?' He also flagged two secondary criteria: (a) ESG/sustainability compliance integration ('we spend a lot of money in sustainability... highly mentioned') and (b) supplier onboarding that accommodates local SMEs with limited digital capability ('local companies don't have this kind of integration... if suppliers are not there we are back to square one'). These criteria are confirmed from Danny's stated needs but have NOT been validated with other stakeholders (IT, finance, exec sponsor) or formalized into a scored evaluation matrix. The workshop planned for 9 Jun (now past) was intended to deepen these criteria with the broader team; post-workshop validation is the next step.

ai_fit_signal.summary: Press Metal is AI Curious. Danny stated 'I have no problem in using AI... we have a few initiatives related to AI in the background... I'm thinking one step ahead' and articulated use cases that autonomous agents directly solve (eliminate manual quotation gathering, automate supplier price input, shift team from tactical to strategic). However, no AI mandate, no AI-specific budget, and no executive-level AI strategy surfaced. Danny's urgency is procurement pain and manual-process waste, not AI adoption for its own sake. AI is a lever he is open to, not the driver. AIS fields are null (no AI score recorded in Salesforce). Based on call content, tier is AI Curious: receptive, forward-looking, sees AI as an enabler, but the deal is anchored to procurement ROI, not AI excitement.

Two buyer calls recorded (30 Mar intro no-show rescheduled, 7 Apr discovery with Danny Lau). Pain and scope confirmed, pricing envelope discussed, workshop planned with multi-stakeholder team. Deal is engaged and moving but early-stage (Qualified, no EB, no competitor intel, no quantified ROI yet).

items.detail: Economic Buyer unmapped and not engaged. Danny (DM) must advocate upward for RM 1.2M budget approval to 'the managers/bosses' but the EB/CFO has not been identified or engaged, and no executive sponsor exists. Without EB sponsorship, deal stalls at DM advocacy.

No quantified ROI or business case built yet, but Danny explicitly stated this is THE approval gate: 'The bosses will ask okay we're gonna spend 1,200,000 where's the value... can you get savings from your supplier 1,200,000? If I can't get that number then what's the point?' Generic 3-5% savings claim is not enough; must build Press Metal-specific model anchored to their spend and manual-process waste.

Competitive evaluation underway with unknown vendors and no intel. Danny stated 'I am also looking at one other supplier portal by next week or so' and confirmed evaluating 'multiple vendors,' but no competitor name, shortlist, or decision timeline disclosed. Risk: silent RFP or point-solution vendor wins on price without Zycus knowing the field.

Close date 30 Oct (126 days) is optimistic for early Qualified stage with EB sign-off, contracting, InfoSec, and multi-stakeholder validation still ahead. Danny stated urgency ('this quarter if possible'), but realistic enterprise motion (workshop → demo → evaluation → commercials → EB approval → contracting) typically runs 6-9 months from discovery. Date will likely slip to Q1 2027 unless dramatically accelerated.

Single-threaded risk: only Danny engaged across both calls; IT (Jonathan Goh), finance, exec sponsor, and supply-chain evaluator (Terrance Chai) not yet on calls despite workshop planning. Workshop 9 Jun intended to bring in broader team, but post-workshop stakeholder engagement unknown.

However, Danny does not control budget (must advocate upward for RM 1.2M approval), has not yet introduced Zycus to the Economic Buyer or exec sponsor, and is evaluating multiple vendors (no exclusivity). items: Positive: Danny Lau (DM) owns pain, stated urgent timeline ('this quarter if possible'), and is driving next steps (workshop coordination, deck share, stakeholder assembly). Source: Avoma call 7 Apr 2026; Next_Step__c.
Positive: Workshop confirmed 1 Jun for 9 Jun execution, signaling forward motion and buyer commitment to detailed evaluation. Source: Next_Step__c.
Positive: Danny described fast internal approval process ('if the managers all agree it's done on the spot') and confirmed budget can be secured if ROI proven. Source: Avoma call 7 Apr 2026.

Negative: Only Danny engaged across both calls; IT (Jonathan Goh), finance, exec sponsor, and supply-chain evaluator (Terrance Chai) not on calls despite being listed contact roles. Multi-threading incomplete. Source: Salesforce OpportunityContactRole; Avoma calls 30 Mar and 7 Apr.
Negative: No Economic Buyer identified or engaged; Danny does not control budget and must advocate upward. No CFO, no exec sponsor. Source: Avoma call 7 Apr 2026; Salesforce contact roles.
Negative: No quantified ROI model built yet, but Danny stated this is THE approval gate. Generic 3-5% savings claim insufficient. Source: Avoma call 7 Apr 2026.
Negative: Competitive landscape dark — Danny evaluating 'multiple vendors' including one other (unnamed) supplier-portal provider, no shortlist or timeline disclosed. Source: Avoma call 7 Apr 2026.

Negative: 13 days since last Salesforce activity (13 Jun); post-9 Jun workshop outcome not yet recorded in Next_Step or calls. Rep outreach 9 Jun awaiting buyer reply per server pulse. Source: LastActivityDate, server pulse.
confidence_signals.summary: Deal is live, engaged, and progressing but thin on multi-stakeholder validation and competitive intel. Positive signals: Danny (DM) owns pain, stated urgency, drove discovery call, requested deck, coordinated workshop, and confirmed fast approval process if ROI proven. Workshop confirmed 1 Jun for 9 Jun execution (planning active). Two buyer calls recorded. Negative signals: only Danny engaged (IT/finance/exec not on calls), no EB identified, no quantified ROI built, no competitor intel, and 13 days since last SF activity with no post-workshop outcome recorded yet. Deal is not cooling (workshop planning is forward motion), but it is early-stage and under-validated. Confidence is moderate: engaged buyer and clear pain, but structural gaps (EB, ROI, competition) and thin stakeholder coverage.

north_star_verdict.math: Days to close: 126 (close 30 Oct 2026). Stage: Qualified (early). Time-in-stage: 80 days since creation (7 Apr 2026). Latest activity: 13 days ago (last SF activity 13 Jun). Planned next milestone: workshop confirmed for 9 Jun (completed ~17 days ago per Next_Step 1 Jun entry), agenda planning in progress. Remaining path: detailed demo → multi-stakeholder evaluation → commercials/pricing → EB/CFO sign-off → MSA/SOW contracting → InfoSec review → close. At Qualified stage with no EB identified, no pricing finalized, no contract initiated, and 4+ months of complex enterprise motion ahead, the close date is credible only with rapid progression. No slip yet, but pace required is aggressive for a $150k enterprise SaaS deal with multi-approval chain.
evidence: 7 Apr 2026: Initial corporate deck presented to Danny Lau (Head of Supply Chain & Procurement, Decision Maker contact role) — discovery call establishing pain (manual processes, resource waste, need for automation) and timeline (Danny wants solution 'this quarter' if possible). Source: Avoma call 7 Apr, Next_Step__c.
1 Jun 2026: Workshop confirmed for 9 Jun, agenda planning in progress per Next_Step update. Source: Next_Step__c.
13 Jun 2026: Last Salesforce activity date (13 days ago). Source: LastActivityDate.

Danny articulated clear pain on 7 Apr call: 'We are wasting a lot of resources where it should not be. We should be looking in the market trying to get the best price. But instead we are working on a lot of these manual tasks that I think can be simplified.' Full source-to-pay automation need confirmed, end-to-end procurement modernization scope. Source: Avoma transcript 7 Apr 2026.

No Economic Buyer identified or engaged across both calls; Danny (DM) owns supply chain/procurement but deferred finance involvement ('I cannot answer for finance... but if there's value in it, why not?'). Budget authority unclear — Danny stated 'I don't have a budget in place, but if the need is there I can get it done' and described fast approval process if managers agree on ROI. No CFO or executive sponsor engaged yet. Source: Avoma call 7 Apr 2026.

Pricing envelope discussed: $300-400k USD annually (~RM 1.2M) SaaS model for full suite, but Danny emphasized ROI hurdle: 'The bosses will ask okay we're gonna spend 1,200,000 where's the value... can you get savings from your supplier 1,200,000? If I can't get that number for them then what's the point?' Quantified value case not yet built. Source: Avoma call 7 Apr 2026.

No competitor named or evaluation process disclosed across both calls; Danny mentioned 'one other supplier portal' vendor visiting 'next week' (context: mid-Apr 2026 timeframe from call date) but no name given. Competitive landscape unknown. Source: Avoma call 7 Apr 2026.

Deal is live and buyer-engaged: workshop planning active, Next_Step shows progression from April through June, and Danny's stated urgency ('if I can have it fast I'll prefer this quarter') signals intent. However, genuine enterprise complexity (multi-stakeholder, ROI validation, EB sign-off, contracting) makes Oct close unlikely without dramatic acceleration. Source: Next_Step__c, Avoma calls 30 Mar and 7 Apr 2026.
north_star_verdict.headline: Deal stalled 13 days post-workshop (9 Jun) with no follow-up demo or evaluation activity. Danny engaged strongly in discovery but no Economic Buyer mapped, multi-vendor eval underway, and momentum lost after initial champion interest.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline (accurate) — deal is genuinely early-stage discovery with workshop planning underway; forecast category matches reality, but close date needs extension to Q1 2027 to align with realistic enterprise motion timeline.

best_practice_check.summary: Deal is healthy and engaged at early Qualified stage but has three structural gaps that will block progression: (1) Economic Buyer is unmapped — Danny (DM) must advocate upward for RM 1.2M budget approval but the EB/CFO has not been identified or engaged, and no executive sponsor exists. (2) Quantified ROI is the explicit approval gate Danny stated, but no Press Metal-specific business case has been built yet — the 3-5% savings claim is generic, not anchored to their $500M+ spend and manual-process waste. (3) Competitive landscape is dark — Danny is evaluating multiple vendors including one other (unnamed) supplier-portal provider, but Zycus has no intel on the shortlist, timeline, or what would make us the winner. Additionally, multi-threading is thin (only Danny engaged; IT/finance/exec not on calls despite workshop planning), and the 30 Oct close date is optimistic for a 126-day runway with EB sign-off, contracting, and InfoSec still ahead.

competitive_position.summary: Competitive landscape is largely opaque. Danny Lau stated on 7 Apr that Press Metal is evaluating 'multiple vendors' and mentioned one other supplier-portal provider scheduled to visit mid-Apr 2026 (no name given). No formal RFP, no disclosed shortlist, no named competitors across both calls or Salesforce. The incumbent alternative is 'do nothing / stay manual' — Press Metal runs Oracle ERP (live 2026) but procurement is fully manual with no procurement software layer, which Danny explicitly rejected as untenable. Zycus positioned as end-to-end source-to-pay (sourcing, contract, supplier portal, eProcurement, eInvoicing, AI agents) with strong Malaysia presence (15 customers, Maybank/Tenaga/DRB-HICOM references) and SME supplier onboarding capability Danny flagged as critical. However, without competitor names, evaluation timeline, or shortlist criteria, competitive strategy is blind. The single current threat is the unnamed supplier-portal vendor Danny was reviewing in mid-Apr; status unknown (no follow-up mention). Risk: if Press Metal runs a silent RFP or selects a lower-cost point solution without Zycus knowing the field, we lose by default. Immediate move: ask Danny directly who else is being evaluated, what the timeline is, and what would make Zycus the winner.

competitors.name: Manual / Do Nothing
competitors.quote: In terms of procurement we are still to me it's still a very manual process... we are wasting a lot of resources where it should not be. We should be looking in the market trying to get the best price but instead we are working on a lot of these manual tasks that I think can be simplified. That's what I'm after.
competitors.how_we_win: Danny has explicitly rejected staying manual; the pain is confirmed and urgent. Position Zycus as the only way to shift his team from tactical to strategic procurement and quantify the ROI (3-5% spend savings on $500M+ annual spend = RM 15M+ potential) to make the business case undeniable.

competitors.name: Unknown supplier-portal vendor
competitors.quote: I am also looking at one other what they call it supplier portal by next week or so. They propose something that's quite interesting the idea.
competitors.how_we_win: Danny described this as a 'supplier portal' (narrower scope than Zycus full source-to-pay). Differentiate on breadth (end-to-end sourcing, contract, eProcurement, eInvoicing vs point solution), AI/agentic automation (40% tactical work reduction), Malaysia proof (15 local customers, SME supplier onboarding), and Oracle integration depth (60%+ of Zycus customers run SAP/Oracle). Co-build the quantified ROI case and secure the workshop win to position Zycus as the strategic platform vs a tactical portal. Urgent: discover this vendor's name, status, and Danny's evaluation timeline.

ai_positioning_strength.score: Moderate — AI positioning resonated and aligns with buyer vision (elevate team to strategic, automate tactical work), but decision hinges on ROI and procurement transformation, not AI mandate.
ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI/agentic positioning was prominent in the 7 Apr discovery call. Zycus led with the agentic AI platform (121 autonomous agents, 40% tactical work reduction, Merlin intake via Teams natural language), and Danny responded positively: 'I have no problem in using AI... we have a few initiatives related to AI in the background... I'm thinking one step ahead.' Danny articulated the exact use case AI agents solve: eliminating manual tactical work ('I want my team to spend time getting strategic... I don't want to spend time on day to day') and automating supplier price input and PO creation. However, AI was positioned as a FEATURE of the platform, not the WEDGE. Danny's decision criteria center on ROI and end-to-end automation, with AI as an enabler. Press Metal is AI-curious (initiatives underway, Danny wants to move ahead of internal AI projects) but not AI-hungry (no AI mandate or AI-specific budget). Score: moderate strength. AI resonated and aligns with Danny's vision, but the deal will be won on quantified procurement savings and process transformation, not AI alone. AI should remain a proof point (40% efficiency, autonomous agents) in the ROI narrative, not the lead.

items.quote: I'm looking for everything actually... the sourcing part, the automation, the matching on invoices... trigger back into Oracle or Netsuite to continue issuance of the PO.
items.criterion: End-to-end source-to-pay automation integrating with Oracle ERP

items.quote: I want the supplier to use the portal to input their prices because we buy a lot of things that is fluctuating on a day to day basis the price. It changes from time to time.
items.criterion: Supplier portal with real-time pricing for volatile commodities (daily price fluctuations)

items.quote: Local companies SME or smaller they don't have this kind of integration. So if you want to integrate something very powerful in Press Metal but in the end the suppliers are not there we are back to square one.
items.source: Avoma call 7 Apr 2026, Danny Lau; Zycus positioned OCR invoice capture from handwritten notes and shared supplier network as solution
items.criterion: Supplier onboarding that accommodates local SMEs with limited digital capability

items.quote: We also have compliance sustainability ESG procurement... I want to have this to help us achieve that as well. Press Metal is one of the top companies in Malaysia that we are focusing on sustainability. We spend a lot of money in the sustainability side.
items.source: Avoma call 7 Apr 2026, Danny Lau; Zycus mentioned supplier risk/performance module but did not detail ESG scoring or compliance workflow — must validate in workshop

items.quote: What is the value it brings? Can you get savings from your supplier 1,200,000? If I can't get that number for them then what's the point? The bosses will ask okay we're gonna spend 1,200,000 where's the value.
items.source: Avoma call 7 Apr 2026, Danny Lau; Zycus mentioned 3-5% typical savings but no Press Metal-specific ROI model built yet — this is THE approval gate
items.criterion: Quantified ROI and spend savings to justify RM 1.2M annual investment

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Press Metal's stated needs align well with Zycus's full source-to-pay platform, with three strong fit areas and two areas requiring validation. Strong fit: (1) End-to-end procurement automation from sourcing through invoice matching integrating with Oracle ERP — Zycus core capability, 60%+ customers on SAP/Oracle. (2) Supplier portal with real-time pricing for volatile commodities and support for both contracted and spot pricing — Zycus supplier network and portal module directly address this. (3) Local SME supplier onboarding with low digital-maturity tolerance — Zycus positioned OCR invoice capture, simplified onboarding, and shared supplier network as solving this. Validation needed: (1) ESG/sustainability compliance integration — Danny flagged this as strategic priority but Zycus did not detail ESG module capability on the call; must confirm coverage in workshop. (2) Multi-location procurement decisioning (East vs West Malaysia sourcing based on urgency and price) — Danny described manual decisioning on every req; unclear if Zycus workflow supports location-based routing rules. ROI is the gate: Danny repeatedly emphasized that approval hinges on quantified savings ('can you get 1,200,000 ringgit savings?'), and Zycus mentioned 3-5% spend savings as typical but did not yet build Press Metal's specific business case. Workshop outcome and ROI model will determine fit credibility.

hard.ais_why:
• Across 243 chars of NextStep covering two dated entries from Apr to May 2026 ‚Äî corporate deck presentation and demo planning ‚Äî zero mention of AI, Merlin, agentic or any AI capability. Product scope contains no AI SKUs (iContract; iSupplier; eProcurement; eInvoicing; iSaaS; iRisk). Per the 7 Apr 2026 note: 'Presented corporate deck on 7th April to Danny - Head of supply chain & procurement.'
• On 22 Apr 2026: 'Working with Danny for Demo date in the first week of May. Demo date- To be scheduled for May 2026 as per prospect availability.' Danny is the named Head of Supply Chain & Procurement; SFDC confidence is 20%. The engagement has not progressed past initial corporate-deck presentation and there is no AI-related dialogue, RFP scope, or buyer ask captured.
• Confidence is Low because the NextStep is very thin (243 chars, two notes). With no AI products in scope and only a corporate-deck presentation completed, score 2 reflects an early-stage traditional S2C engagement with no AI signal in any direction.

packets.subject: Koon Ming

value.name: Manual / Do Nothing
value.quote: In terms of procurement we are still to me it's still a very manual process... we are wasting a lot of resources where it should not be. We should be looking in the market trying to get the best price but instead we are working on a lot of these manual tasks that I think can be simplified. That's what I'm after.
value.how_we_win: Danny has explicitly rejected staying manual; the pain is confirmed and urgent. Position Zycus as the only way to shift his team from tactical to strategic procurement and quantify the ROI (3-5% spend savings on $500M+ annual spend = RM 15M+ potential) to make the business case undeniable.

packets.subject: Manual / Do Nothing

value.name: Unknown supplier-portal vendor
value.quote: I am also looking at one other what they call it supplier portal by next week or so. They propose something that's quite interesting the idea.
value.how_we_win: Danny described this as a 'supplier portal' (narrower scope than Zycus full source-to-pay). Differentiate on breadth (end-to-end sourcing, contract, eProcurement, eInvoicing vs point solution), AI/agentic automation (40% tactical work reduction), Malaysia proof (15 local customers, SME supplier onboarding), and Oracle integration depth (60%+ of Zycus customers run SAP/Oracle). Co-build the quantified ROI case and secure the workshop win to position Zycus as the strategic platform vs a tactical portal. Urgent: discover this vendor's name, status, and Danny's evaluation timeline.

packets.subject: Unknown supplier-portal vendor

value.detail: Economic Buyer unmapped and not engaged. Danny (DM) must advocate upward for RM 1.2M budget approval to 'the managers/bosses' but the EB/CFO has not been identified or engaged, and no executive sponsor exists. Without EB sponsorship, deal stalls at DM advocacy.
value.source: Avoma call 7 Apr 2026, Danny: 'I don't have a budget in place but if the need is there I can get it done... if the managers all agree it's done on the spot.'

packets.subject: Economic Buyer unmapped and not engaged. Danny (DM) must advocate upward for RM 1.2M budget approval to 'the managers/bosses' but the EB/CFO has not been identified or engaged, and no executive sponsor exists. Without EB sponsorship, deal stalls at DM advocacy.

value.detail: No quantified ROI or business case built yet, but Danny explicitly stated this is THE approval gate: 'The bosses will ask okay we're gonna spend 1,200,000 where's the value... can you get savings from your supplier 1,200,000? If I can't get that number then what's the point?' Generic 3-5% savings claim is not enough; must build Press Metal-specific model anchored to their spend and manual-process waste.

packets.subject: No quantified ROI or business case built yet, but Danny explicitly stated this is THE approval gate: 'The bosses will ask okay we're gonna spend 1,200,000 where's the value... can you get savings from your supplier 1,200,000? If I can't get that number then what's the point?' Generic 3-5% savings claim is not enough; must build Press Metal-specific model anchored to their spend and manual-process waste.

value.detail: Competitive evaluation underway with unknown vendors and no intel. Danny stated 'I am also looking at one other supplier portal by next week or so' and confirmed evaluating 'multiple vendors,' but no competitor name, shortlist, or decision timeline disclosed. Risk: silent RFP or point-solution vendor wins on price without Zycus knowing the field.

value.detail: Close date 30 Oct (126 days) is optimistic for early Qualified stage with EB sign-off, contracting, InfoSec, and multi-stakeholder validation still ahead. Danny stated urgency ('this quarter if possible'), but realistic enterprise motion (workshop → demo → evaluation → commercials → EB approval → contracting) typically runs 6-9 months from discovery. Date will likely slip to Q1 2027 unless dramatically accelerated.
value.source: Authoritative SF close date 30 Oct 2026; Avoma call 7 Apr 2026 discovery timeline

Workshop 9 Jun intended to bring in broader team, but post-workshop stakeholder engagement unknown.

value.flag: Map and engage the Economic Buyer (CFO or senior exec) within 14 days — Danny does not control the RM 1.2M budget and must advocate upward to 'the managers/bosses' for approval; without EB sponsorship this deal stalls at DM advocacy. Ask Danny directly: who approves this investment, and will they join the next meeting to hear the ROI case?

value.flag: Co-build the quantified ROI model with Danny this week — he explicitly stated approval hinges on proving spend savings ('can you get 1,200,000 ringgit savings?'), and the generic 3-5% claim is not enough. Gather Press Metal's annual spend, manual-process FTE hours, and cycle-time baselines, then model the savings in ringgit terms tied to their pain (resource waste, missed strategic sourcing). This is THE gate to executive approval.

value.flag: Discover the competitive field and evaluation timeline within 7 days — Danny is reviewing multiple vendors including one other supplier-portal provider (name unknown, mid-Apr visit, status unknown). Without knowing who we are up against, the evaluation process, or what Danny's decision criteria weigh most heavily, competitive strategy is blind. Ask Danny: who else is being evaluated, what is the shortlist and timeline, and what would make Zycus the clear winner over the alternatives?

scope

gaps: Economic Buyer / CFO not identified or engaged — Danny (DM) must advocate upward for RM 1.2M budget approval but EB has not been mapped; no exec sponsor contact role.

Competitive landscape opaque — Danny evaluating 'multiple vendors' including one other (unnamed) supplier-portal provider; no competitor name, shortlist, or decision timeline disclosed; Salesforce Competitors__c empty.

Post-9 Jun workshop outcome not recorded — workshop confirmed 1 Jun for 9 Jun execution per Next_Step, but no call, task, or Next_Step update captures outcome, stakeholder feedback, or next steps 17 days later.

Multi-stakeholder validation incomplete — IT (Jonathan Goh), finance, and supply-chain evaluator (Terrance Chai) listed as contact roles but have not appeared on any call; only Danny engaged across both touchpoints.

No quantified ROI model built — Danny explicitly stated ROI proof is THE approval gate ('can you get 1,200,000 ringgit savings?'), but only generic 3-5% savings mentioned, no Press Metal-specific business case.

Paper process (contracting, legal, InfoSec) completely unknown — no MSA, SOW, legal review, InfoSec gate, or procurement mechanics discussed; two partner-domain contacts (Hwan Lim, Koon Ming) listed but role unclear.
